channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Earthquakes near Greek Island Leave Residents on Edge
item.description: A series of earthquakes near Santorini have authorities shut down schools, dispatch rescue teams with sniffer dogs, and advise residents to drain swimming pools.
item.dc:creator: Demetris Nellas, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Big Tech on the rise one year after Senate beat-down
item.description: With new dynamics emerging between Silicon Valley and Washington, some advocates for stronger social media regulations and parents are concerned their cause will get lost in the dust despite past momentum.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: India's richest person wants to build the world's largest data center, five times the capacity of Microsoft's biggest site
item.description: Mukesh Ambani wants to build the world's largest data center, five times the capacity of Microsoft's mega site.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia giving away free AI courses worth up to $90 and no, it's got nothing to do with DeepSeek's ascension
item.description: Nvidia’s free AI courses offers a golden opportunity for developers to master generative AI, deep learning, and accelerated computing at no cost.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Everything new on Disney Plus in February 2025: Pixar's Win or Lose, A Thousand Blows, and more
item.description: From Pixar's first-ever TV show to more episodes of Marvel's new Spider-Man series, here's what to expect on Disney Plus this month.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI begins releasing its next generation of reasoning models with o3-mini
item.description: OpenAI released its newest reasoning model, called o3-mini, on Friday. OpenAI says the model delivers more intelligence than OpenAI’s first small reasoning model, o1-mini, while maintaining o1-mini’s low price and speed. The company says o3-mini excels in science, math, and coding problems.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: The Mysterious Disappearance of Hailey Welch: The Rise and Fall of Hawk Tuah Girl
item.description: The sudden disappearance of Hailey Welch, also known as Hawk Tuah girl, has left fans wondering about her safety and well-being.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky explains how he helped Sam Altman during OpenAI’s 2023 board fiasco
item.description: After 17 years, Airbnb’s Brian Chesky is hitting reset—reinventing the business from the ground up and expanding the brand in unexpected ways.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: JavaScript Temporal to ease dates and times
item.description: Web developers will soon find it easier to work with dates and times in JavaScript, thanks to emerging browser support for JavaScript’s Temporal object, according to Mozilla.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Are you problematic? This ‘cancel culture insurance’ could help you deal with social media backlash
item.description: Worried about getting canceled? There’s now insurance policy for that. Insurance agency Samphire Risk is rolling out “cancel culture” insurance for celebrities and execs afraid of social media backlash, according to a report from the Financial Times.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: World's first AI-designed, 3D-printed shoe wants to be the next Crocs
item.description: A startup claims to have developed the world's first entirely artificial intelligence-designed and 3D-printed shoe, the brainchild of a 25-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tesla promises more affordable EVs and could start its Robotaxi service in June – but I've heard this all before
item.description: Elon Musk makes more big Tesla promises during a quarterly earnings call, but I'd like to see it go back to doing what it does best.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: President Trump to meet with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at the White House
item.description: President Donald Trump is meeting Friday with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, whose company designs and supplies the advanced computer chips that play an integral role in developing artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Kevin Freking And Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Snowden speaks out on Gabbard confirmation battle
item.description: Former National Security Agency contractor Edward Snowden defended former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President Trump's nominee to be director of national intelligence, following a fiery confirmation hearing in the Senate on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft makes OpenAI’s o1 model free for Copilot users
item.description: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman writes on LinkedIn that the company is now making OpenAI’s reasoning model o1 free to use for all users of Microsoft’s AI assistant Copilot. Microsoft calls the functionality itself “Think Deeper.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Football lover? You can now win an exclusive Tottenham Hotspur experience when signing up to ExpressVPN
item.description: ExpressVPN kicked off its partnership with the Spurs back in November and this is the first engagement campaign. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Cómo DeepSeek cambia la ecuación de la IA generativa para los CIO
item.description: La empresa emergente china de inteligencia artificial (IA) DeepSeek causó sensación la semana pasada cuando dio a conocer una versión de código abierto de su modelo de razonamiento, DeepSeek-R1, que afirma tener un rendimiento superior al modelo generativo preentrenado (GPT) o1 de OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The quantum computing reality check
item.description: In late 2024, a major pharmaceutical company invested $50 million in quantum computing initiatives through a leading cloud provider, hoping to revolutionize their drug discovery process.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Simple, secure device retirement
item.description: Is your IT service provider’s warehouse overrun by your depreciating end-of-life IT assets, and they don’t know what to do with them? Do your work-from-home and on-the-go employees have old devices lying around collecting dust and posing a security risk? Retire devices securely and responsibly—and a
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How Apple Vision Pro is finding a home in healthcare
item.description: In early 2024, Ryan Broderick, a surgeon at UC San Diego Health, was commiserating with some colleagues about the profusion of screens in today’s operating rooms. Though the displays provided essential guidance for minimally invasive operations in progress, they also added complexity.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: After a week of DeepSeek freakout, doubts and mysteries remain
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Apple's iPhone sales during the holiday season slipped despite a highly anticipated AI rollout
item.description: Apple on Thursday disclosed its iPhone sales dipped slightly during the holiday-season quarter, signaling a sluggish start to the trendsetting company’s effort to catch up to the rest of Big Tech in the race to bring artificial intelligence to the masses.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Timeline of HPE’s $14 billion bid for Juniper
item.description: AI is a key factor in one of the tech industry’s most significant M&A developments: HPE’s proposed acquisition of Juniper Networks. The $14 billion deal would shake up the industry, and regulatory watchdogs in the U.S. and worldwide have raised antitrust concerns.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim
item.description: "Data centers is one of the new industrial sectors that we've been looking at because it's part of the emerging economy and new technologies," said Arman Shehab The post Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CompTIA unveils AI Essentials training resource
item.description: Artificial intelligence is changing how work gets done, creating new technology roles, and transforming the entire employment landscape.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to organize, protect and streamline your inbox with an email alias
item.description: Creating an email alias can help clean up your inbox by organizing emails based on purpose, like creating different aliases for shopping, newsletters and work.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump World learns how to deal with Musk
item.description: President Trump’s allies and aides are learning to deal with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, who has had a consistent presence in the early days of the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Industry coalition presses DOGE to act on digital identity
item.description: Digital identity advocates are calling on Trump administration to boost the use of mobile drivers licenses and other digital identity verification use cases. The post Industry coalition presses DOGE to act on digital identity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Doing authentication right
item.description: Any substantial website is going to need to customize itself for individual users. Thus, it is going to have to authenticate those users — that is, let them log in. As a software developer, it is your job to make the whole process as smooth and secure as possible.
item.dc:creator: Info World
